"ok" is usually capitalized as "OK"

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (1, 21, 31, 41, 51, 61, 71, 81, 101, 1001, …), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.
Formatting %d followed by words ("scans"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("receipts"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("days"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("days"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("more"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("more"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("more"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("free"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
issue.errorLine1: <string name="add_scans_left">%1$d of %2$d free scans left (photo or file). Manual entry is always free.</string>

issue.message: Formatting %d followed by words ("free"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
issue.errorLine1: <string name="add_scans_used">You\'ve used all %1$d free scans. Go Premium to scan more — manual entry is always free.</string>

issue.message: Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
issue.errorLine1: var dueDay by remember { mutableStateOf(original?.dueDay ?: if (isIncome) 25 else 1) }

issue.message: Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
issue.errorLine1: var color by remember(key) { mutableStateOf(original?.colorArgb ?: Categories.defaultColor) }

Assigning `Locale.getDefault()` to a final static field is suspicious; this code will not work correctly if the user changes locale while the app is running
Assigning `Locale.getDefault()` to a final static field is suspicious; this code will not work correctly if the user changes locale while the app is running
issue.errorLine1: val MONTH_YEAR: DateTimeFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("MMM yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

issue.message: Assigning `Locale.getDefault()` to a final static field is suspicious; this code will not work correctly if the user changes locale while the app is running
